preferred_translation: one breathes in remembering the Dhamma; one breathes out remembering the Dhamma
alternative_translations: one breathes in remembering the Dhamma and breathes out remembering the Dhamma
discouraged_translations: mindfully one breathes in, mindfully one breathes out
one breathes in mindfully and out mindfully
definition: Shared MN 10 / MN 118 breathing line keeping sati explicit as remembering the Dhamma while breathing in and out.
notes: Use this record for the shared breathing-side line in MN 10 and MN 118. Keep sati explicit as remembering the Dhamma rather than generic mindfulness or arbitrary recollection. This line is a governed control phrase for the repo's practice-text surface and should stay aligned across both discourses.

example_phrases.translation: one breathes in remembering the Dhamma; one breathes out remembering the Dhamma
example_phrases.notes: Shared breathing-side line in MN 10 and MN 118.

example_phrases.translation: one breathes in remembering the Dhamma; one breathes out remembering the Dhamma
example_phrases.notes: Same governed line in the breathing section of MN 10.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the shared breathing-side line as the repo's governed control phrase for both the MN 10 breathing section and the MN 118 training sequence.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the shared breathing-side formula in MN 10 and MN 118 unless a recorded local reason requires minor punctuation adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not collapse remembering into mindfulness-jargon or leave the remembered content implicit when the repo wants the Dhamma-remembering force to stay explicit.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the breathing-side sati line from drifting into generic mindfulness wording or losing the Dhamma-remembering force that governs the practice surface.
